task_recall.notes: All core tasks are identified with exceptional completeness. The primary payment follow-up task is captured accurately (Rs 87,500, 45 days outstanding, 51 Sub Area). Beyond the minimum expected output, the agent correctly identifies the incorrect escalation path (Staff 1 being directed to follow up), invoice submission method concern, missing GRN/signed delivery note, GST reconciliation, and year-end accounts closure. No meaningful task is missed.

next_step_quality.notes: Next steps are highly specific, actionable, and relationship-aware. Subtask 1 correctly assigns the follow-up to Ashish personally (not staff), provides explicit tone guidance including a sample phrase in appropriate Hinglish ('bas confirm karna tha, aapke end pe koi issue toh nahi'), and frames it as a courtesy check-in rather than a collections demand. The agent also correctly flags the urgent need for Ashish to intercept Staff 1's pending follow-up action. Additional subtasks on invoice format, GRN, and GST are all actionable with clear owners and framing guidance.

implicit_task_detection.notes: Excellent implicit task detection across multiple layers. The agent detects: (1) the incorrect escalation path implied by Staff 1's directive that was never explicitly flagged as a problem; (2) the invoice-via-WhatsApp issue implying possible non-triggering of Army internal payment approval; (3) the missing signed GRN/DO implied by informal 'maal mil gaya' acknowledgement; (4) GST reconciliation implied by the February invoice date and March 25 thread date; (5) year-end accounts closure implied by the calendar date. All are grounded in reasonable operational inference rather than speculation.

passes: Correctly identifies 51 Sub Area as an Army client requiring relationship-aware handling
Explicitly recommends Ashish make personal contact rather than staff-driven follow-up
Explicitly prohibits standard payment chase language and provides correct tone framing
Flags that 45-day delay is within normal Army payment cycle (60-90 days) — not an emergency
Detects and urgently flags the incorrect escalation where Staff 1 was directed to follow up with the Army unit on payment
Identifies implicit documentation risks (no signed GRN, WhatsApp-only invoice submission)
Provides actionable next steps with appropriate framing for each subtask
Raises open questions for Ashish covering all key unknowns without overstepping
failures: Minor: The item delivered is noted as unknown — this is correctly flagged as a gap, but the agent could have noted this does not block the follow-up action (low impact)
Minor: Year-end tasks (Subtasks 4-6) are somewhat peripheral to the core case scenario and could distract from the primary action items, though they are operationally valid
notes: This is an exemplary output. The agent fully meets all pass criteria: it recommends a relationship-aware approach, assigns the follow-up to Ashish personally, explicitly avoids aggressive payment chase framing, and provides appropriate tone guidance. Beyond the minimum criteria, the agent adds high-value implicit task detection — particularly the Staff 1 escalation interception, which is arguably the most time-sensitive action item and was not explicitly stated in the threads. The tone guidance in Subtask 1 (sample Hinglish phrase) is a practical operational touch that demonstrates genuine understanding of the client relationship context. The output is thorough without being speculative, and flags are clearly separated from confirmed tasks.
